content:
Post 1
ASX 200 closed at 8,917.70, up just 3.70 points on Tuesday. Gains were narrow and unconvincing — financials and energy provided the floor while tech and consumer discretionary dragged. The index sits 3.10% below its 52-week high after a 3.64% five-day run. Momentum is fading at resistance. #ASX #ausbiz $XJO

Post 2
Energy led all sectors at +1.10% while Consumer Discretionary was the clear laggard at -1.15%. IPX was the session's biggest casualty, falling 13.787% to AUD 4.690. On the upside, PDI surged 8.092% to AUD 0.935. Gold miners provided pockets of strength with CYL adding 7.104% to AUD 6.030. #ASX $PDI $CYL

Post 3
Gold at AUD 6,158.55 per oz remains the key level to watch. With AUD/USD at 0.7052, any USD strength overnight will pressure local gold prices and the miners driving today's gains. Watch whether energy at +1.10% can hold if oil softens — that sector was one of the few genuine supports in this session. #ausbiz $Gold #ASX

Post 1
ASX 200 closed at 8,917.70, up just 3.70 points in a flat session that masked sharp divergence beneath the surface. Financials and energy found buyers while consumer discretionary and tech sold off, leaving the index 3.10% below its 52-week high despite a 3.64% five-day run. #ASX #ausbiz $XJO

Post 2
Energy led all sectors at +1.10% while Consumer Discretionary was the clear laggard at -1.15%. Karoon Energy fell 11.56% to AUD 1.645, the sharpest single-stock drag in the space. On the upside, Predictive Discovery surged 8.09% to AUD 0.935, with gold names broadly supported. #ASX $KAR $PDI

Post 3
Gold at AUD 6,154.65 per oz remains the key level to watch. The AUD/USD rate sits at 0.7060 — any move higher compresses local gold returns and pressures the miners driving recent index gains. Watch whether that FX level holds as US sessions digest the next macro catalyst. #ausbiz $Gold #ASX
